content:
First, shift \(x\) so that the three points of interest become symmetric about 0.  Let
\[
q(t)=p(t+15),
\]
so that
\[
q(-10)=p(5)=A,\quad q(0)=p(15)=B,\quad q(10)=p(25)=C.
\]
Since \(p\) is a cubic with integer coefficients, so is \(q\).  Write
\[
q(t)=\alpha t^3+\beta t^2+\gamma t+B,
\]
with \(\alpha,\beta,\gamma,B\in\mathbb Z\).  Then
\[
q(10)=1000\alpha+100\beta+10\gamma+B,\qquad
q(-10)=-1000\alpha+100\beta-10\gamma+B.
\]
The given condition \(p(5)+p(25)=1906\) becomes
\[
q(-10)+q(10)=1906
\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;
(1000\alpha+100\beta+10\gamma+B)+(-1000\alpha+100\beta-10\gamma+B)=1906
\]
\[
\Longrightarrow 200\beta+2B=1906
\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;
100\beta+B=953
\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;
B=953-100\beta.
\]
Thus
\[
|p(15)|=|B|=\bigl|953-100\beta\bigr|,
\]
and we can choose the integer \(\beta\) to make this as small as possible.  Since \(953\equiv53\pmod{100}\), the closest multiple of 100 to 953 is \(1000\), giving
\[
|953-100\cdot10|=|953-1000|=47.
\]
No smaller absolute value is possible.  Hence the minimum possible value of \(|p(15)|\) is

\[
\boxed{47}.
\]

message.content:

\[
q(t)=p(t+15),

\[
q(10)=1000\alpha+100\beta+10\gamma+B,\qquad
q(-10)=-1000\alpha+100\beta-10\gamma+B.
\]
The given condition \(p(5)+p(25)=1906\) becomes
\[
q(-10)+q(10)=1906
\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;

\[

\[
